inventory: Inventoried: 4 connector groups, 23 connectors, 12 server groups, 8 segment groups, 30 application segments, 14 access policy rules, 3 forwarding policy rules, 2 timeout policy rules, 2 LSS configs.

findings.title: Server group has no app connector group bound
findings.remediation: Bind at least one app connector group to each server group, otherwise traffic destined for the bound application segments will fail to forward. Use the zpa/update-server-group skill.

findings.title: Application segment uses an overly broad wildcard domain
findings.remediation: Replace `*.*` with explicit FQDNs or a `*.<your-domain>` wildcard. A double-wildcard segment will match every DNS query and steal traffic intended for other segments.

findings.title: Access policy is missing a final deny-all rule
evidence: Last rule in policy: rule-allow-corp-vpn-fallback (action ALLOW)
findings.remediation: Add an explicit DENY rule at the bottom of the access policy with no scoping conditions. Without it, evaluation falls through to the implicit allow on some legacy ZCC builds. Use the zpa/create-access-policy-rule skill.

findings.title: POSTURE rule does not cover all five OS platforms
evidence: rule-require-posture-windows (covers: Windows, macOS — missing: iOS, Android, Linux)
findings.remediation: Add the same POSTURE condition for the missing platforms, or scope the rule to a CLIENT_TYPE that excludes them. Otherwise iOS, Android, and Linux clients silently bypass this posture check.

findings.title: Forwarding policy contains an overly broad INTERCEPT rule
findings.remediation: Scope INTERCEPT rules to specific app segments or narrower CIDRs. Intercepting an entire /8 forwards far more user traffic into ZPA than intended and can mask outbound issues.

findings.title: App connector group runs below N+1 redundancy
findings.remediation: Deploy a second connector in each group so a single instance failure does not break access. Live load (CPU / bandwidth) is in the Cannot Audit section — confirm sizing from your hypervisor metrics.

findings.title: Connector enrollment certificates expiring within 30 days
findings.remediation: Re-enroll the affected connectors before expiry to avoid a sudden tunnel drop. The current enrollment cert is exposed on the connector group detail; renewal is automatic for connectors that re-checkin in time.

findings.title: Server group binds every app connector group in the tenant
findings.remediation: Limit each server group to the connector groups that are actually closest to the backend hosts. Binding all groups defeats geo-routing and can produce noisy health alarms.

findings.title: Discovery wildcard segment has health reporting enabled
findings.remediation: Set `health_reporting` to `NONE` on discovery / wildcard segments. Health probing every wildcard target floods the connectors and produces a stream of false negatives.

findings.title: Contractor SCIM group is allowed without a paired block-rest rule
findings.remediation: Add a paired DENY rule for the same SCIM group so that contractors only reach the explicitly allowed segments. Without it, the broader rules below this allow may grant unintended access.

findings.title: Canonical bypass list is incomplete
findings.remediation: Add BYPASS rules for the missing canonical destinations so that Windows Update and OCSP/CRL traffic does not get forwarded into ZPA and stalled by inspection. Use the zpa/create-forwarding-policy-rule skill.

findings.title: Reauth timeout is outside the recommended 24h–7d window
findings.remediation: Reduce `reauth_timeout` to between 24 hours and 7 days, depending on how strict the user re-verification cadence needs to be. 30 days is well outside the baseline window.

findings.title: LSS config streams logs over plain TCP
findings.remediation: Enable TLS on the LSS config and re-deploy the LSS connector with the corresponding root CA. Plain TCP between the LSS connector and the SIEM forwarder is only acceptable inside fully isolated networks.

findings.title: Baseline LSS feeds are not all configured
findings.remediation: Add LSS configs for the missing baseline feeds so the SIEM has a complete picture (user status changes, connector health, and connector metrics). Each feed is a separate LSS config.

findings.title: App connector group is missing geo-routing coordinates
findings.remediation: Populate latitude and longitude on every connector group so that ZCC can pick the closest entry point. The default routing fallback is acceptable but produces longer tail latencies.

findings.title: Application segments addressed only by IP / CIDR
findings.remediation: Migrate IP-only segments to FQDNs once the backend hosts have stable DNS. FQDN segments survive backend re-IPs and pair correctly with dynamic discovery server groups.

findings.title: Deception rule is present in the access policy
findings.remediation: Informational. Deception rules are auto-provisioned when the Deception license is enabled. Do not modify or remove unless coordinating with your Zscaler Deception admin.

findings.title: LSS config has no status-code filter applied
evidence: lss-audit-to-splunk (filter is empty — streams every status code)
findings.remediation: Apply a status-code allowlist to limit catch-all streaming. Streaming every code can inflate SIEM ingest cost without improving signal — start with the codes the SIEM detection rules actually use.

findings.title: Per-connector CPU / memory / throughput
findings.remediation: No telemetry API. Use hypervisor / cloud-provider metrics out-of-band to confirm each connector has headroom — the N+1 check above counts connectors only, not load.

findings.title: App probe results / per-target reachability
findings.remediation: No health API. The audit can only verify the configured `health_reporting` mode, not whether the probes are succeeding. Confirm reachability through the ZPA admin portal.

findings.title: LSS log delivery / drops / SIEM ingestion health
findings.remediation: LSS API is configuration-only. Monitor delivery, drops, and ingest lag from the SIEM side — Zscaler does not surface in-flight LSS telemetry.

findings.title: AC group bandwidth utilization (live N+1 sizing)
findings.remediation: No traffic-stats API. The N+1 check counts connectors only — confirm bandwidth headroom through your hypervisor or cloud provider's network metrics.

findings.title: Active session counts per app segment (idle-timeout sizing)
findings.remediation: No session-stats API. Idle-timeout sizing in this audit is informed by app class only, not measured load. Pull session counts from the ZPA admin portal if you need to tune timeouts to actual usage.
